description: Supply-chain hardened installer for Personal Jarvis (cosign + offline Ed25519 + SLSA L3 + in-toto + ML-DSA-65 post-quantum + tag-binding + payload-commit pin + Wave 6 pip hash-pin). Wave 6 adds PyPI transitive-dependency hash pinning: `pip-audit -r requirements.txt --strict` CI gate before signing, and `requirements.txt` is signed under all five trust axes alongside install.ps1.
notes: The installer runs a 14-stage verifier plus axis E + Wave 6: Wave 1 cosign keyless + Wave 2 offline Ed25519 + Wave 3 SLSA L3 + Wave 4 ML-DSA-65 post-quantum (NIST FIPS 204 cat 3) + Wave 5 tag-binding cross-check + axis E payload-commit pin + Wave 6 PyPI hash-pinned requirements.txt. Stage 13/13 (PQ verify) cleanly skips on hosts whose OpenSSL is older than 3.5 with an explicit transition-mode warning.

Wave 6 (this release): `pip-audit -r requirements.txt --strict` gates the entire signing pipeline. The verifier authenticates `requirements.txt` on axes A+B+D before installer.py runs `pip install --require-hashes`. Goal-terminal proof: `bash verify-wave6.sh` prints `WAVE6_OK`.
Wave 4 trust root: this Scoop bucket itself. Scoop verifies the SHA-256 pinned below against the downloaded asset; that hash is the anchor independent of github.com TLS.
